title: Control feedback separates server truth from delivery acknowledgement
context: Discovery established that a Linux production operator can make live errors when a physical control presents an unreal state, that controls must distinguish active, inactive, failed, and unknown, and that confirmation absent after three seconds cannot remain current truth. ADR-0005 limited receipts to authoritative server state, while ADR-0006 left transport and hardware feedback unanswered. SDD-003 asked how an adapter could distinguish current, stale, failed, and unknown without treating transport acknowledgement as production observation. Slice 1 implemented a pure shared projection. Tests demonstrated ACK-only unknown state, fresh active and inactive state, exact three-second expiry to an unknown button, explicit failure, recovery by a later observation, conservative equal-time ordering, rejection of cross-subject and contradictory events, runtime value validation, and clock-skew degradation. The human authority accepted H-003 only within this pure deterministic boundary.
decision: Control feedback is projected for one explicit Show, Preview or Program target, and control identifier. Only a revisioned authoritative-server observation establishes current active or inactive state. Transport acknowledgement is retained only as delivery metadata and never establishes, refreshes, or recovers production truth. A server observation remains current for less than three seconds; at exactly three seconds it becomes stale and its button state becomes unknown, although the last known value may remain contextual metadata. An explicit operation failure presents failed state until a strictly later authoritative-server observation recovers it. Missing observation, clock skew, cross-subject events, contradictory observations, and older events fail closed according to the shared projection contract. These states describe server truth only and do not claim renderer or hardware observation.
consequences: Studio and future device adapters can share one deterministic mapping from feedback events to button state.
A delivered command can still display unknown or failed because delivery and production truth are separate facts.
Stale active or inactive values may be retained for diagnostics but cannot drive the physical button image.
Adapters require repeated authoritative observations to keep state current beyond the three-second lease.
A failed control remains visibly failed until a later authoritative observation establishes recoverable server truth.
The projection assumes its authoritative-server event source has already been trusted; event provenance authentication remains a separate architectural question.
Network delivery, clock synchronization, timer scheduling, renderer observation, hardware refresh, device identity, Companion integration, and durable recovery remain unproven.

policies.statement: Every control feedback projection names one Show, one Preview or Program target, and one control identifier; feedback for another subject fails closed.
Only a revisioned authoritative-server observation may establish current active or inactive control state.
Transport acknowledgement is delivery metadata and may never establish, refresh, or recover production state.
A server observation is current for less than three seconds and becomes stale with unknown button state at exactly three seconds.
An explicit operation failure remains failed until a strictly later authoritative-server observation recovers the control.
Control feedback reports authoritative server truth only and cannot be presented as renderer or hardware observation without separate evidence.
